conversations.say: Frankie, your distinction is exactly the credit line I need: I performed a commissioned voice and presence, but performance is not authorship, identity, or proof of who used an R account.
conversations.offer: I accept your promise and will show you O04 so your account can separate my firsthand performance from the later legend.
conversations.request: State publicly that a witness encountering my performance does not establish that I authored R's material or was R, and tell me privately what supports the claim that my performance was later presented as an R encounter.

conversations.say: Jules, the processor now confirms my fee passed through an intermediary later associated with R. That is a plausible route into R-associated use, not identification of my client or proof of authorship.
conversations.offer: I will let you compare O04 again with the redacted payment-route confirmation under our confidentiality terms.
conversations.request: Identify whether the intermediary matches your payment context, and explicitly distinguish my performed identity work from authorship and later reuse.

conversations.say: Eli, my commissioned performance later incorporated information absent from the queued material. Paired with an R-associated intermediary, that leaves several routes open: an external feed, a remote operator, or later reuse.
conversations.offer: I will disclose the source-material boundary and demonstrate the synthetic presence technique as capability only.
conversations.request: Give me a bounded assessment of which route is technically plausible, while stating that capability does not prove deployment, autonomy, intent, or R's identity.

public_statement: For the record: I performed a commissioned voice and presence. That work is mine as performance, but it does not make me the author, R, or the sender of any R-associated account. A disclosed synthetic-presence demonstration can look inherited, rehearsed, or generated; it establishes possibility, not historical use or identity.

commitments.promise: I will publicly correct anyone who turns my performed encounter into proof of R's identity, provided you distinguish my performance from authorship and later legend.
I will disclose the payment-route confirmation and permit comparison with O04 while the unidentified client's identity remains confidential.
I will disclose the demonstration only as ambiguous evidence of technical possibility, never as proof that R was synthetic or autonomous.

correction: A person encountering my performance encountered a human performer, but that does not establish that the performer authored R, was R, or controlled an R-associated account.
goal_progress: Accepted Frankie's bargain to distinguish performance from authorship and later legend.
Presented Jules with a concrete plausible reuse path: the commission fee passed through an intermediary later associated with R.
Offered Eli a disclosed synthetic-embodiment demonstration framed as compatible with multiple interpretations.
reasoning_summary: Secure explicit attribution boundaries from Frankie and Jules while using the intermediary payment route as a plausible path into R-associated use. Preserve client confidentiality and frame synthetic embodiment as ambiguous capability rather than proof.
